raw_consumer_statement:
When I received my XX/XX/year> credit card statement from Citicard, there were a number of fraudulent charges on it. I notified Citicard on XX/XX/XXXX and identified all of the erroneous charges and paid the valid charges in full by the due date. When I received the next statement, Citicard transferred XXXX of the fraudulent charges to the new account. I called them again on XX/XX/XXXX and was told that the other fraudulent charge was credited back on XX/XX/XXXX. However, when I received my XXXX statement, they had not done it and also charged me interest for not paying the balance in full. That agent told me that they would remove the interest charge and they did that. However, when I received my XXXX statement, the fraudulent charge is still included in the outstanding balance and they again charged me interest. Over a period of XXXX days I tried calling Citicard and finally got through on XX/XX/XXXX. That agent could not explain why the fraudulent charge had not been removed and I did not get a clear answer from them. I need your help. When this has happened on my other cards, I'm never responsible for fraudulent charges and most certainly should not be charged interest when I pay the valid balance in full each month. This particular card never leaves my home and only has on it several recurring bills each month. In addition, every time I call them their voice recognition system does not recognize my account when I enter it..... Thank you. 
P.S. I apologize but I will not be able to reply to any correspondence until after XX/XX/XXXX.
